mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 payroll growth of 175k persons indicates sustained labor demand
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with improving labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.11% and CPIAUCSL at 231.5 indicate accommodative monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite tightening signal, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) above 0.5% signals accelerating consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through immediate earnings revision upside.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 RSAFS at 421.1 billion dollars shows consumer spending momentum
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth months do not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than negative growth months (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.2% while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling supply-side weakness and recessionary pressure that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change < -0.2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 INDPRO at 98.67 and UNRATE at 7.6% show stable supply conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite supply weakness signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 800,000 annualized units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized, indicating demand-side weakness that reduces forward S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through lower construction and real estate sector earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 800k annualized AND GDPC1 growth < 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 HOUST at 853k and Q1 2013 GDPC1 growth indicates moderate demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances despite demand weakness signal, or correlation between HOUST-GDPC1 weakness and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 7.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 persons month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through earnings forecast downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 UNRATE at 7.6% and PAYEMS growth of 175k indicate labor market strength
regime_hint: labor market weakness or stagnation phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite labor weakness signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.25% signals monetary easing that compresses equity discount rates and increases forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through valuation expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.11% reflects zero-rate policy environment
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or zero-rate environment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable (monthly change < 0.2%), signaling healthy supply expansion without inflationary pressure that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.4% AND CPIAUCSL monthly change < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 INDPRO at 98.67 and CPIAUCSL at 231.5 show stable supply-price dynamics
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite supply acceleration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
